title: Session restore: non-auth backend error shows retry screen and preserves persisted token

preconditions.description: Playwright runs Expo Web using baseURL http://localhost:8081 (playwright.config.ts webServer).
Before page load, localStorage contains 'auth_token' = DATA-1 so app/index.tsx attempts session restore.
Before page load, localStorage contains 'onboarding_complete' = 'true' so successful routing targets home (not onboarding) on retry success.
Playwright intercepts GET **/users/me with a two-attempt sequence: attempt 1 returns a non-auth error (e.g., 503 with JSON error envelope), attempt 2 returns 200 with a CLIENT user.
preconditions.notes: Validator implementation note: use a request counter inside page.route('**/users/me', ...) to fulfill different responses on first vs second invocation.

preconditions.description: Playwright intercepts GET **/users/me/app-state with a two-attempt sequence: attempt 1 returns non-auth error (503), attempt 2 returns 200 with nextAction=IDLE (CLIENT).
preconditions.notes: On attempt 1, app/index.tsx may call /users/me/app-state from the fallback path inside the catch block (after /users/me fails). On attempt 2, syncAndRoute will call /users/me/app-state after /users/me succeeds.

testData.name: persistedAuthToken
testData.notes: Seeded into localStorage key 'auth_token' before the app loads.

journey.notes: App loads SessionRestore (app/index.tsx) which reads persisted token and attempts GET /users/me first.

target.text: Cannot reach server. Please try again.

journey.notes: Non-auth error path should render ErrorScreen with this exact message (app/index.tsx).
After clicking Retry, restore() runs again and should re-attempt /users/me and then /users/me/app-state.
On retry success (attempt 2), resolved route for IDLE CLIENT should be /(client)/home → web URL /home.

target.text: Cannot reach server. Please try again.

assertions.notes: Non-auth errors must not clear persisted token; app/index.tsx only logs out on 401/403.

notes: This design explicitly uses ONLY existing endpoints referenced in code/contract: GET /users/me and GET /users/me/app-state.

If the validator prefers not to implement a two-attempt mock, it may stop after Assertion 3 (Retry visible + token preserved). The sequence-based approach is recommended because it validates the Retry actually recovers.
